text: The surgeon makes a circular incision around the terminal ileum and takes down the ileostomy. A 12mm trocar is inserted into the defect, and pneumoperitoneum is established for exploratory laparoscopy. Multiple adhesions between the small bowel, peritoneum, and rectal stump are visible and are released using an ultrasonic dissector.
The surgeon performs a mesorectal excision, sealing the rectal vessel separately using Hem-o-lok clips.

The surgeon completes the dissection of the rectum down to the levator muscles. A recto-digital examination is performed, revealing a very low dissection. The rectal stump, previously attached to the abdominal wall, is released.

The surgeon laparoscopically staples off the distal rectum using a GIA-Fortify device. The small bowel is assessed and deemed insufficient for pouch creation and IPAA. The rectum and ileum are grasped with instruments and exteriorized through a lower abdominal incision. Another firing of the GIA-Forty-Five stapler is used to remove the ileal segment leading towards the ileostomy.

The surgeon oversews the dead end of the J-pouch using a running suture, followed by a leak test that shows no leakage. The head of a 29mm circular stapler is inserted into the tip of the pouch, which is then reintroduced into the abdominal cavity. The shaft of the stapler is positioned into the short anal rectal stump, creating the ileal pouch-anal anastomosis without tension. A subsequent leak test confirms the absence of leaks.
